finding.contract_claim: Parameter `plumbing_result` is declared in the signature of build_scorecard(config, analysis_results, junk_results=None, dead_code_results=None, plumbing_result=None) (signature at lines 106-113) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: No call site ever passes `plumbing_result`: the only caller, run_audit in src/osoji/audit.py, invokes build_scorecard with config, analysis_results and junk_results only, so the parameter always takes its default None and the branches gated on it never execute.

finding.triage_reasoning: Same reasoning as Claim 8. `plumbing_result` is also annotated `# Backward compat:` and is referenced in 8 internal sites in scorecard.py, but the only known caller (audit.py's run_audit) never passes it. The sweep covered only 2 files, leaving open whether external library consumers pass it. The multiple internal branches using it (L237-L322) would never execute within the observed call graph. Verdict: uncertain pending a broader caller sweep.
finding.suggested_fix: If a full codebase search confirms zero callers pass `plumbing_result`, add a deprecation warning and schedule removal of the backward-compat paths.
